task.name: Microecon Platform: Foundational Capabilities
task.description: Build rigorous theoretical foundations (Phase 0) and agent belief architecture (Phase 1) as prerequisites for institutional comparison research. Phase 0 verifies existing implementation matches canonical microeconomic theory. Phase 1 adds memory and configurable beliefs so agents can learn from observations.

vision_extraction.primary_goal: Research-first agent-based microeconomics platform making institutions (bargaining, information, search) explicit, configurable, and comparable
success_criteria: Enables personal research as productive instrument
Generates publications with novel results or methodology
deliverables: Verified theoretical foundations matching canonical texts
Agent belief architecture supporting market emergence research

context.gap_analysis_summary: Phase 0: ~60% coverage exists but scattered in functional tests; need dedicated tests/theory/ verification layer. Phase 1: 0% exists; greenfield requiring design ADR, new beliefs module, agent extension, search/exchange integration.

primary: Kreps I: Microeconomic Foundations I (preferences, demand, equilibrium)
Kreps II: Microeconomic Foundations II (bargaining, information)
O&R-G: A Course in Game Theory
theoretical_references.phase_1_specific: Kreps I Ch 5-7 (Choice under Uncertainty, Utility for Money, Dynamic Choice)

phases.name: Theoretical Alignment
phases.description: Verify that current implementation matches theoretical foundations from canonical texts. Create dedicated tests/theory/ directory as verification layer (existing functional tests remain). Document any divergences found.
phases.exit_criteria: All tests/theory/ tests pass with specified tolerances; no unresolved divergences (or divergences documented as acceptable); user approval via AskUserQuestion before proceeding to Phase 1

phases.name: Agent Belief Architecture
phases.description: Extend agent model to support integrated beliefs over prices and opponent types. Requires design ADR reviewed against Kreps I Ch 5-7 and approved by user before implementation. Beliefs must be configurable with interface supporting multiple update rule implementations.
phases.exit_criteria: ADR approved; all belief tests pass; simulation runs with belief-enabled agents without regression; full test suite green

features.name: Verify Nash Bargaining Maximizes Nash Product
features.description: Add tests verifying NashBargainingProtocol correctly maximizes the Nash product (u1-d1)(u2-d2). Test that no feasible allocation achieves higher Nash product than computed solution.

functional: Test computes Nash product at solution
Test verifies no feasible perturbation increases Nash product
Test covers symmetric and asymmetric preference cases
property: Solution maximizes Nash product over feasible set

features.name: Verify Rubinstein SPE and BRW Weights
features.description: Add tests verifying RubinsteinBargainingProtocol computes correct SPE allocation per BRW (1986). Verify bargaining weights match formula: w1 = ln(d2)/(ln(d1)+ln(d2)).

functional: Test BRW weight formula directly
Test first-mover advantage magnitude matches theory
Test convergence to symmetric Nash as delta -> 1
property: Weights sum to 1
More patient player gets larger weight

features.name: Verify Utility and Preference Calculations
features.description: Add tests verifying CobbDouglas preferences match textbook formulas. MRS = (alpha/(1-alpha)) * (y/x). Marshallian demand x* = alpha*M/px, y* = (1-alpha)*M/py.

functional: Test MRS formula across range of bundles
Test Marshallian demand formula
Test indirect utility computation
Test boundary cases (x=0, y=0)
property: MRS strictly decreasing along indifference curve
Demand exhausts budget
Homothetic property: budget shares constant

features.name: Verify Gains from Trade Computation
features.description: Add tests verifying gains from trade are computed correctly and fully exhausted at bargaining solution. At solution, no remaining bilateral gains should exist.

functional: Test total surplus = sum of individual utility gains
Test gains exhausted at solution (no further improvement possible)
Test gains computed correctly for various preference/endowment combinations
property: Gains non-negative for individually rational trades
Gains zero when agents identical (no trade needed)

features.name: Verify Pareto Efficiency of Outcomes
features.description: Add tests verifying bargaining outcomes are Pareto efficient. No feasible reallocation should Pareto-dominate the solution. MRS equality at solution.

functional: Test no Pareto-dominating allocation exists (grid search)
Test MRS equality at solution
Test outcome lies on contract curve
property: Pareto efficiency of Nash solution
Pareto efficiency of Rubinstein solution

features.name: Document and Resolve Divergences
features.description: Compile findings from THEORY-001 through THEORY-005. Document any divergences between implementation and theory. Fix implementation issues or document acceptable divergences with rationale.

functional: Divergences documented in markdown file
Each divergence has resolution status (fixed/acceptable/pending)
Acceptable divergences have explicit rationale
property: No unresolved divergences blocking Phase 1

features.escalation: Any divergence found triggers AskUserQuestion before resolution

features.name: Design Belief Architecture ADR
features.description: Create Architecture Decision Record for belief system. Must cover: belief representation (price beliefs, partner beliefs), memory structure, update rule interface. Review against Kreps I Ch 5-7. Requires explicit user approval before implementation proceeds.

functional: ADR document exists at docs/current/ADR-BELIEF-ARCHITECTURE.md
Covers price belief representation with rationale
Covers partner/type belief representation
Covers memory structure (what stored, depth, eviction)
Defines update rule interface (ABC/Protocol)
Lists concrete implementations to build (Bayesian, heuristic)
property: Design grounded in Kreps I Ch 5-7 decision theory
Interface supports multiple update rule implementations
Memory structure is serializable for logging
theory: Kreps I Ch 5: Choice under Uncertainty
Kreps I Ch 6: Utility for Money

features.approval_method: AskUserQuestion tool presenting ADR summary, requiring explicit confirmation

features.name: Implement Agent Memory
features.description: Extend Agent class with memory/observation history. Trade history, price observations, partner interaction history. Configurable memory depth.

functional: Agent has memory attribute storing observations
Trade history records (partner_id, bundle_exchanged, tick)
Price observations recorded from visible trades
Memory depth configurable (last N observations or all)
Memory serializable for logging
property: Memory does not leak (respects configured depth)
Memory persists across ticks within simulation
Adding observation is O(1)

features.name: Implement Belief Update Logic
features.description: Create beliefs module with update rule interface and multiple implementations. Interface (ABC) with at least two concrete implementations: Bayesian (conjugate priors where tractable) and heuristic (exponential moving average).

functional: BeliefUpdateRule ABC/Protocol defined
BayesianUpdate implementation (conjugate priors)
HeuristicUpdate implementation (EMA with configurable alpha)
Price belief and partner type belief support
Update methods take observation, return updated belief
property: Bayesian posterior matches analytical formula (where tractable)
EMA converges to true value given consistent observations
Beliefs bounded to valid ranges
theory: Bayesian updating: P(theta|data) proportional to P(data|theta)P(theta)

features.name: Integrate Beliefs with Search
features.description: Update search logic to incorporate beliefs. Target evaluation uses beliefs about partner types (not just observed types). Price expectations influence surplus calculation.

functional: Search can use believed types for surplus calculation
Agents with different beliefs make different target choices
Search respects information environment (beliefs about observed types)
Configurable: belief-based vs. observation-based search
property: Belief-based search reduces to observation-based when beliefs = observations
Search decisions deterministic given beliefs

features.name: Integrate Beliefs with Exchange
features.description: Update bargaining to incorporate beliefs. Agents use believed types for strategy (not true types when info asymmetric). Beliefs update post-trade based on revealed information.

functional: Bargaining protocols can accept believed types
Post-trade belief updates occur
Information revelation during bargaining configurable
Works with existing NashBargainingProtocol and RubinsteinBargainingProtocol
property: Beliefs converge toward truth with repeated interaction
No regression in bargaining correctness (theory tests still pass)

features.name: Belief System Integration Tests
features.description: Comprehensive integration tests for belief architecture. Full simulation with belief-enabled agents. Verify no regression in existing functionality.

functional: Simulation runs with belief-enabled agents
Beliefs update correctly across ticks
Logging captures belief state
Batch runs work with belief configuration
property: All 450+ existing tests still pass
No performance regression >10%
Belief state serializable in logs
theory: System behavior consistent with bounded rationality framework

resolved.choice: Additive: create tests/theory/ as verification layer, keep existing functional tests
resolved.rationale: Separation of concerns; functional tests for behavior, theory tests for mathematical correctness

resolved.choice: ADR-first: design document reviewed against Kreps I Ch 5-7, user approval required before implementation
resolved.rationale: Foundational architecture requires careful design; avoid rework

resolved.choice: Configurable from day one: interface with multiple implementations (Bayesian, heuristic)
resolved.rationale: Agent sophistication is experimental variable per VISION.md; need comparison capability

resolved.choice: Sequential: Phase 0 must complete entirely before Phase 1 begins
resolved.rationale: Phase 1 builds on verified foundation; no point adding beliefs to incorrect bargaining

resolved.choice: Tiered: strict (rel=1e-6) for formulas, moderate (rel=1e-3) for optimization outcomes
resolved.rationale: Formulas have exact answers; optimization involves numerical search with inherent tolerance

escalation_triggers: Any theory divergence found (THEORY-001 through THEORY-005) -> AskUserQuestion before resolution
Phase 0 complete -> AskUserQuestion for approval before starting Phase 1
BELIEF-001 ADR complete -> AskUserQuestion for approval before implementation
Design ambiguity where multiple valid approaches exist -> AskUserQuestion
Existing test failure caused by changes -> AskUserQuestion before proceeding

loop_parameters.commit_convention: Conventional commits with scope (e.g., 'test(theory): add Nash product maximization test')
loop_parameters.status_update_location: This PRD file: update feature status fields as work progresses
